questions.scenario: The API subnet has the expected route table, but the route for partner traffic points to the wrong next-hop IP. What is the narrow fix?

options.text: Repair the specific route entry for the partner CIDR.
Move the whole API into a new VNet.
Delete the NSG so routing is easier to test.

questions.explanation: The evidence names one route entry. A targeted route repair is safer than moving the app or changing unrelated boundaries.

questions.scenario: A deploy fails because the subnet lacks the delegation required by Azure Container Apps. Which layer is responsible?
questions.explanation: Delegation is part of subnet configuration. It controls which Azure service can place resources there.

questions.scenario: What should be verified after changing a route table attached to the API subnet?

options.text: The route table still has the expected entries.
The API can reach its required private and outbound dependencies.
The user-facing path still reaches a healthy backend.
Only that the change saved successfully in Azure.

questions.explanation: Saving a route is not enough. The team needs route evidence plus application reachability evidence.
